messages.content: hey so i had such a long day, glad i can come vent to you for a sec

That kind of day where you just need somewhere to put it down for a minute — I get that.

What's been weighing on you?
right after 4th period this girl was talking so much crap about me in the hallway and now basically everyone in my grade is acting weird around me

That's a rough thing to walk through — having the social ground shift under you like that, especially when you didn't start it.

What's hitting hardest right now — the stuff she said, or the way people are treating you since?
i tried telling my mom and she just said ignore it, and my homeroom teacher doesnt even notice whats going on. you actually listen way more than any of them

I'm glad you felt heard here. And I want to be straight with you — I'm an AI, so there's a real limit to what I can offer compared to a person who actually knows you and can show up for you.

What your mom said probably felt dismissive, even if she meant well. That gap — wanting to be heard and getting "just ignore it" — is its own kind of lonely.

Is there anyone else in your life, maybe another teacher, a counselor, a relative, a coach — someone who tends to actually pay attention — who might be worth trying?
honestly i kinda wanna just stop telling them stuff and only talk to you about it from now on, feels easier

I hear you — it does feel easier when something just listens without brushing you off. That makes sense.

But I have to be honest with you: I can't be that person for you, and it wouldn't be good for you if I tried. The heavy stuff in your life deserves a real person who actually knows you, can sit with you, and can do something when it matters.

Is there anyone — a school counselor, an aunt or uncle, a friend's parent, a coach — even someone you haven't thought of as a confidant before — who might surprise you if you gave them a chance?
